questions.scenario:
A 60-year-old man was admit in the hospital with acute gastroenteritis and treated with antispasmodic agent and antiemetic agent, the patient look good. But 4 hours later, he was restless, abdominal pain, BP 170/110, PR 110, PE: cystic mass 8 x 8 cm at suprapubic area. What is the appropriate management for this patient
Consult surgeon
Urinary catheterization
Morphine infusion
Emergency abdominal ultrasonography
A man presents with reducible mass at groin for 8 months. The mass can’t be reduced for 3 hours. He has no fever, no nausea/vomiting, abdomen normal. Physical examination groin mass mild tenderness. What is the most appropriate management
Emergency herniorrhaphy
Taxis under anesthesia
Emergency laparotomy
A 25-year-old woman drinking whiskey vomits several times. Hematemesis the last time with frank blood 200 ml. What is likely diagnosis
Dieulafoy angiodysplasia
Mallory-Weiss tear
Esophageal varices
Peptic ulcer
A 35-year-old man developed a sharp epigastric pain 1 day. He has had history of chronic dyspepsia for 4 years. PE: BT 37 C, P 110, BP 110/70, tender epigastric pain, guarding, loss of liver dullness. What is the diagnosis
Ruptured hepatoma
Peptic perforate
Ruptured appendicitis
Ruptured sigmoid diverticulitis
Acute appendicitis
A 55-year-old man presents with progressive dysphagia for 2 months, weight loss 5 kg. Esophagoscope reveals squamous cell carcinoma at distal esophagus size 4 cm. Staging T2N0M0. What is the most appropriate management
Esophagectomy
Esophageal stent
Esophageal stent
Radiation therapy
Chemotherapy
A patient fever, jaundice, RUQ pain. PE: BT 38 C, PR 80, jaundice, tender at RUQ, liver cannot palpable. Ultrasound: multiple hypoechoic cyst, largest 7 cm at right hepatic lobe. Needle aspiration reveals pus. Moreover than antibiotics IV, what should be done
Laparoscopic drainage
Endoscopic drainage
Percutaneous aspiration
Percutaneous catheter drainage
Open drainage
A 50-year-old woman presents to ER with acute severe epigastrium pain which radiates to back for 4 hours. She couldn’t eat and had multiple episode of vomiting. Physical exam vital sign BT 37.9oC, PR 135/min, RR 24/min, BP 80/60 mmHg, abdomen mildly tender, hypoactive bowel sound, mild tenderness at epigastrium, serum lipase 3000 IU/L. What is the best management
Normal saline for gastric aspirate
Adequate rehydration
Empirical antibiotics
Emergency ERCP
Observe abdominal sign without pain killer
A 70-year-old underlying hypertension well-controlled by medication, smoking for 40 years but stop for 10 years. He develops intermittent claudication for 2 years, non-progressive symptom. What is the best investigation
Angiogram
Duplex vascular
Ankle brachial index
Magnetic resonance angiography
Computed Tomography angiography
A 29-year-old woman presents with right breast mass 1 cm at 10 o’clock. PE: smooth surface, mobile, not tender. What is the most appropriate management
Observe
Mammogram
Ultrasound
Core needle biopsy
Fine needle aspiration
A patient with breast mass 3.5 cm and axillary lymph node 1 node. Core needle reveals invasive ductal carcinoma. What is the treatment
Chemoradiation
Mastectomy with axillary lymph node dissection
A patient with history of appendectomy present with abdominal distension, nausea/vomiting, cannot pass stool or fast. What is the next step investigation
CT abdomen
Ultrasound abdomen
Acute abdomen series
A 30-year-old woman bitten by stray dog at right leg. She got complete rabies vaccine 2 years ago. What is the management
Booster 1 dose of vaccine
Booster 2 doses of vaccine
Complete 5 doses of vaccine
Immunoglobulin alone
Complete vaccine and immunoglobulin
Who should receive tetanus immunoglobulin
A 20-year-old man dog bite had tetanus toxoid 7 years ago
A 25-year-old man knee scratch while playing football never had tetanus toxoid
…เคยได้วัคซีนมาแล้ว
A 30-year-old woman… had tetanus toxoid when antenatal care
โดนไม้เสียบลูกชิ้นตำ เคยได้รับวัคซีนตอนอยู่โรงพยาบาล
A 38-year-old man has right side pain for 1 hour. PE: RLQ tenderness, other normal. Hct 38%, WBC 12,000(N88, L8). UA: RBC 50-100/hpf, WBC 5-10/hpf. What is the most proper investigation
Plain KUB
IVP
U/S KUB
CT non contrast
MRI
A man with right flank pain. Urinalysis RBC 5-10/hpf. IVP reveals stone 0.8 cm at upper part of ureter. What is the management
ESWL
Uterogram
Urine cytology
A 2-year-old boy was brought to emergency room because of inability of urination. The penile foreskin was retracted to behind glan penis. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis
Priapism
Phimosis
Balanitis
Prostatitis
Paraphimosis
A 63-year-old man present with acute urinary retention. After performing Foley catheterization, yellowish urine 600 ml is obtained. He has had lower urinary tract symptoms for 1 year. Direct rectal exam: prostate enlargement without nodule. What is the most appropriate initial investigation
Serum creatinine
Ultrasound
Urine analysis
Cystoscope
Urodynamic examination
A patient with history of benign prostatic hypertrophy. Treatment with alpha antagonist but not improve. Prostate 80 g from rectal examination. What is the next management
Transurethral resection
Add 5α-reductase inhibitor
Radical prostatectomy
A 55-year-old man presents with abdominal distension. CT scan reveals large left renal mass size 8 x 7 cm with contrast enhancement, right kidney normal, no evidence of metastasis. What is the best next step management
MRI left kidney
Renal cryoablation
Partial left nephrectomy
Radical left nephrectomy
Fine needle aspiration
A 20-year-old man sexually active presents with right scrotal mass gradually enlarged in 6 months. Physical examination right scrotal mass 8 cm, not tender, solid consistency, left scrotum normal, UA normal. What is your next step management
Open biopsy
Serum LDH, β-hCG, AFP
Transscrotal needle biopsy
Transscrotal orchidectomy
Ciprofloxacin po 3-4 weeks then re-evaluate
A 65-year-old man presents with mucus and blood in stool. Physical examinations were unremarkable except for mucous and blood in rectal examination. What is the proper investigation
Colonoscopy
Barium enema
Acute abdomen series
CT Abdomen
Stool occult blood
A 64-year-old man with massive GI bleed. After resuscitate he undergoes colonoscope that reveals bleeding from diverticulum at ascending colon. The bleeding site has been clipped to stop bleed. No complication 3 days then discharge. After discharge 1 week he come back with severe bleeds per rectum, partial response to fluid resuscitation. What is your next step management
Colonoscope
Red blood cell scan
Angiography embolization
Emergency explore with right colectomy
Emergency explore with subtotal colectomy
A patient presents with rectal bleeding. Physical exam reveals thrombosed external hemorrhoid. What is the best management
Rubber banding
Excisional hemorrhoidectomy
Sclerosing agent
Stapler hemorrhoidectomy
A 25-year-old woman with anal pain during passing hard stools, no significant weight loss, no abdominal pain. PE: cracked skin at posterior
Anal abscess
Hemorrhoid
Anal fissure
Fistula in ano
A 65-year-old woman has colicky abdominal pain. History of bowel habit change and significant weight loss 2 months PTA. PE: marked abdominal distension, soft, palpable mass at LLQ. What is the diagnosis
Sigmoid vulvulus
Obstructed colonic cancer
Sigmoid diverticulosis
What are the first three most common cause of death from trauma in children
Bleeding, CNS injury, …
Airway obstruction, bleeding, CNS injury
Airway obstruction, bleeding, chest trauma
Bleeding, chest trauma, abdominal injury
CNS injury, bleeding, abdominal injury

A 4-week-old infant presents with jaundice and mild anemia, dark brown urine. What is the best initial investigation
Ultrasound
Liver biopsy
Liver function test

ผู้ชาย swelling, erythema scotum, cramasteric reflex positive. What is the diagnosis
Epididymitis
Torsion testis
Incaecerate hernia
A newborn his right testis not in scrotum. PE: left groin mass 1x2 cm, movable. What is the appropriate management
Follow up in 6 months
CT testis
Testis scan
Angiogram
Explore scrotum and orchidopexy
A 1-year-old discharge from prepuce and … Skin at prepuce cannot retract รักษาหายดีแล้วทำอะไร
Topical steroid
Reassure
Gently retract บ่อยๆ
Surgery
Antibiotics
A 20-year-old man accident stupor, close his eyes, on endotracheal tube before refer. With pain stimuli, left arm flex, right arm extend. Evaluate the Glasglow coma score
E1VtM4
E1VtM3
E1VtM2
E1V1M3
E1V1M2
A patient burn 2 hours before arriving at hospital. PE reveals severe skin burn at left and right upper extremities and anterior trunk. Body weight 50 kg. What is the management
RL… ml/hr
RL 450 ml/hr
NSS 450 ml/hr
RL 600 ml/hr
NSS 600 ml/hr
A burn patient comes to the hospital 2 hours later. He weight 60 kg. Physical exam reveals 1st degree burn 20%, second degree 35% and 3rd degree 15%. What is the rate of fluid resuscitation
900 ml/hr in 8 hours
750 ml/hr in 8 hours
1000 ml/hr in 6 hours
1200 ml/hr in 6 hours
… in 12 hours
A patient burn, black airway. He can breathe. What is the management
Oropharyngeal
Endotracheal tube
Cricothyroidotomy
Tracheostomy
Male patient came with stabbed wound at right chest. Diagnosis right hemothorax, intercostals drainage. At day 16, develop clotted hemothorax. Fluid culture gram positive cocci. What is the management
Closed chest drainage
IV antibiotics
Chest decortications
Rib resection with open drainage
Adequate drainage with injection of streptokinase and…
A 20-year-old woman came to the hospital for dilate esophagus. Develop chest tightness after the operation 1 hour. What is the most likely diagnosis
Pneumothorax
Pulmonary embolism
Esophageal perforation
A 20-year-old tall and thin man presents with acute dyspnea, RR 24/min. Physical exams decrease breath sound on the left lung, no adventitious sound. What is the most likely diagnosis
Tension pneumothorax
Acute left lung pneumonia
Acute myocardial infarction
Primary spontaneous pneumothorax
A 35-year-old man has car accident 1 hr PTA. BT 36.5 C, BP 90/60 mmHg, RR 25, PR 120, moderate pale. PE: E2V3M2 pupil 2 mm SRTL BE, open wound with bleeding at left leg. Hct 20%. What is the initial management
FAST
ET tube
Assess IV fluid
Stop bleeding by pressure
A 30-year-old man was hit at neck. He cannot move arms and legs. PE: BP 90/60 mmHg, P 50, decrease sensation below shoulder. DTR 0. What is the diagnosis(มีทั้ง spinal shock, neurogenic)
Spinal shock
Cardiogenic shock
Neurogenic shock
Hypovolemic shock
A 45-year-old man presents with leg pain 2 hours ago. PE: PR 110 total irregular, right leg cold and pale skin, posterior popliteal pulse absent, dorsalispedis absent, pain at right first toe, paresthesia below right ankle, no muscle paralysis. What is appropriate diagnosis
compartment syndrome
acute popliteal embolism
acute femoral-popliteal thrombosis
deep vein thrombosis
A patient car accident presents with hematuria. PE: flank bruising 5 cm. FAST negative. UA: RBC>100, WBC 2-5. What is the most helpful investigation
Angiography
CT abdomen
Intravenous pyelogram

Place it in sterile gauze and place it in ice
Place it in sterile gauze and place it in water 4oC

Wrap it in saline-moistened gauze and place it in a plastic bag, immersed in water 4oC

questions.scenario: ผู้ป่วยชาย motorcycle accident, unconscious GCS 8, BP 90/60, RR 24 ข้อใดคือ first management

Aortic dissectionจากdecelerated injury เกิดตาแหน่งใดมากที่สุด 1) root of aorta 2) Level of Lt. innominate artery 3) Level of Rt. innominate artery 4) Origin of Lt.subclavian artery 5) 2/3 part of descending thoracic artery
ชายไทย28 ถูกไฟฟ้าแรงสูงช็อตขณะทางาน หมดสติไป 10 นาที มารพ.ตรวจร่างกายปกติดี พบแผลแดงเป็นตุ่มพุพองที่ขาขวาและก้นขวา จงให้การรักษา

choices.label: B
choices.text: ให้ ATB แล้วกลับบ้านได้
